core_model.structure_summary: An interconnection of maximal monotone (set-valued, generalized-nonexpansive) operators whose periodic steady behavior is characterized as the zero/fixed point of a sum operator and computed by a splitting forward-step fixed-point iteration.
core_model.canonical_form: \text{find } x:\; y=S(x),\; S=S_1+S_2 \text{ maximal monotone};\qquad x^{j+1}=x^{j}-\alpha\,S(x^{j}),\quad \alpha=m/L^2,\quad S=(1-\theta)I+\theta G,\quad G(x)=M^{\top}F(Mx)

variables.meaning: signal/vector sought as fixed point of the operator iteration (a periodic trajectory)
maximal monotone operator (generalization of a convex subgradient) whose zero is sought
iteration step size set by monotonicity and Lipschitz constants
interconnection/incidence operator coupling the component operators
periodic continuous time over one period, plus discrete iteration index of the solver

notes: Extraction is noisy. Core structure is maximal-monotone-operator interconnection theory: the periodic behavior of a port interconnection is a fixed point of a monotone operator, solved by an operator-splitting forward-step iteration that respects the interconnection graph. Two candidate framings: (a) the static maximal-monotone operator inclusion (could be 'algebraic'), (b) the fixed-point iteration itself ('discrete_update'). Chose 'optimization' because maximal monotone operators and their splitting solvers are the paper's optimization-theoretic core.
